([/ləˈmɒ̃/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); French: [[lə mɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Fr-Le_Mans.ogg "File:Fr-Le Mans.ogg")) is a [city](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France") in northwestern [France](/wiki/France "France") on the [Sarthe River](/wiki/Sarthe_(river) "Sarthe (river)") where it meets the [Huisne](/wiki/Huisne "Huisne"). Traditionally the capital of the [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_France "Provinces of France") of [Maine](/wiki/Maine_(province) "Maine (province)"), it is now the capital of the [Sarthe](/wiki/Sarthe "Sarthe") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") and the seat of the Roman Catholic [diocese of Le Mans](/wiki/Diocese_of_Le_Mans "Diocese of Le Mans"). Le Mans is a part of the [Pays de la Loire](/wiki/Pays_de_la_Loire "Pays de la Loire") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France").

**Le Mans** ([/ləˈmɒ̃/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); French: [[lə mɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Fr-Le_Mans.ogg "File:Fr-Le Mans.ogg")) is a [city](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France") in northwestern [France](/wiki/France "France") on the [Sarthe River](/wiki/Sarthe_(river) "Sarthe (river)") where it meets the [Huisne](/wiki/Huisne "Huisne"). Traditionally the capital of the [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_France "Provinces of France") of [Maine](/wiki/Maine_(province) "Maine (province)"), it is now the capital of the [Sarthe](/wiki/Sarthe "Sarthe") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") and the seat of the Roman Catholic [diocese of Le Mans](/wiki/Diocese_of_Le_Mans "Diocese of Le Mans"). Le Mans is a part of the [Pays de la Loire](/wiki/Pays_de_la_Loire "Pays de la Loire") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France").

Its inhabitants are called *Manceaux* (male) and *Mancelles* (female). Since 1923, the city has hosted the [24 Hours of Le Mans](/wiki/24_Hours_of_Le_Mans "24 Hours of Le Mans"), the world's oldest active endurance sports car race. The event is among the most attended and [prestigious motor sports events](/wiki/Triple_Crown_of_Motorsport "Triple Crown of Motorsport") in the world.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [Timeline of Le Mans](/wiki/Timeline_of_Le_Mans "Timeline of Le Mans")

First mentioned by [Claudius Ptolemy](/wiki/Claudius_Ptolemy "Claudius Ptolemy"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) the [Roman](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire") city *Vindinium* was the capital of the [Aulerci](/wiki/Aulerci "Aulerci"), a sub tribe of the [Aedui](/wiki/Aedui "Aedui"). Le Mans is also known as *Civitas Cenomanorum* (City of the [Cenomani](/wiki/Aulerci_Cenomani "Aulerci Cenomani")), or *Cenomanus*. Their city, seized by the Romans in 47 BC, was within the ancient [Roman province](/wiki/Roman_province "Roman province") of [Gallia Lugdunensis](/wiki/Gallia_Lugdunensis "Gallia Lugdunensis"). A 3rd-century [amphitheatre](/wiki/Amphitheatre "Amphitheatre") is still visible. The *[thermae](/wiki/Thermae "Thermae")* were demolished during the [crisis of the third century](/wiki/Crisis_of_the_Third_Century "Crisis of the Third Century") when workers were mobilized to build the city's defensive walls. The ancient wall around Le Mans is one of the most complete circuits of [Gallo-Roman](/wiki/Gallo-Roman "Gallo-Roman") city walls to survive.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

As the use of the French language replaced late [Vulgar Latin](/wiki/Vulgar_Latin "Vulgar Latin") in the area, *Cenomanus*, with [dissimilation](/wiki/Dissimilation "Dissimilation"), became known as *Celmins*. *Cel-* was taken to be a form of the French word for "this" and "that", and was replaced by *le*, which means "the".

As the principal city of [Maine](/wiki/Maine_(province_of_France) "Maine (province of France)"), Le Mans was the stage for struggles in the eleventh century between the counts of [Anjou](/wiki/County_of_Anjou "County of Anjou") and the dukes of [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy "Normandy"). When the Normans had control of Maine, [William the Conqueror](/wiki/William_the_Conqueror "William the Conqueror") successfully invaded England and established an occupation. In 1069 the citizens of Maine revolted and expelled the Normans, resulting in [Hugh V](/wiki/Hugh_V_of_Maine "Hugh V of Maine") being proclaimed [count of Maine](/wiki/List_of_Counts_and_Dukes_of_Maine "List of Counts and Dukes of Maine"). [Geoffrey V of Anjou](/wiki/Geoffrey_V_of_Anjou "Geoffrey V of Anjou") married [Matilda of England](/wiki/Matilda_of_England "Matilda of England") in the [cathedral](/wiki/Le_Mans_Cathedral "Le Mans Cathedral"). Their son [Henry II Plantagenet, king of England](/wiki/Henry_II_of_England "Henry II of England"), was born in the town.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In the 13th century Le Mans came under the control of the French crown. It was subsequently invaded by England during the [Hundred Years' War](/wiki/Hundred_Years%27_War "Hundred Years' War").[[6]](#cite_note-britannica.com-6)

Industrialization took place in the 19th century which saw the development of railway and motor vehicle production as well as textiles and tobacco manufacture.[[6]](#cite_note-britannica.com-6)

[Wilbur Wright](/wiki/Wilbur_Wright "Wilbur Wright") began official public demonstrations of the airplane he had developed with his younger brother [Orville](/wiki/Orville_Wright "Orville Wright") on 8 August 1908, at the Hunaudières horse racing track near Le Mans.[[7]](#cite_note-hc-7)

### World War II

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: World War II")]

Just outside Le Mans city centre there used to be an [airfield](/wiki/Le_Mans_Airfield "Le Mans Airfield"), built to protect the [Renault](/wiki/Renault "Renault") factory.

Soon after Le Mans was liberated by the U.S. [79th](/wiki/79th_Infantry_Division_(United_States) "79th Infantry Division (United States)") and [90th](/wiki/90th_Infantry_Division_(United_States) "90th Infantry Division (United States)") Infantry Divisions on 8 August 1944,[[8]](#cite_note-8) engineers of the [Ninth Air Force](/wiki/Ninth_Air_Force "Ninth Air Force") Engineering Command began construction of a combat [Advanced Landing Ground](/wiki/Advanced_Landing_Ground "Advanced Landing Ground") on a location the Germans had used a simple airfield. The airfield was declared operational on 3 September and designated as "[A-35](/wiki/Le_Mans_Airfield "Le Mans Airfield")". It was used by several American fighter and transport units in additional offensives across France; the airfield was closed in November 1944.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Main sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Main sights")]

* Le Mans has a well-preserved old town (*Cité Plantagenêt*, also called *Vieux Mans*) and the [Cathédrale St-Julien](/wiki/Cath%C3%A9drale_St-Julien "Cathédrale St-Julien"), dedicated to [St Julian of Le Mans](/wiki/St_Julian_of_Le_Mans "St Julian of Le Mans"), who is honoured as the city's first bishop.
* Remnants of a [Roman](/wiki/Ancient_Rome "Ancient Rome") wall are visible in the old town and Roman baths are located by the river. These walls are highlighted every summer (July and August) evening in a light show that tells the history of the town.
* [Arboretum de la Grand Prée](/wiki/Arboretum_de_la_Grand_Pr%C3%A9e "Arboretum de la Grand Prée")
* [Notre-Dame de la Couture](/wiki/Notre-Dame_de_la_Couture "Notre-Dame de la Couture"), medieval church
* [Notre Dame de Sainte Croix](/wiki/Notre_Dame_de_Sainte_Croix,_Le_Mans "Notre Dame de Sainte Croix, Le Mans"), neogothic church
* Part of the former Cistercian abbey de l'Epau, founded by [Queen Berengaria](/wiki/Berengaria_of_Navarre "Berengaria of Navarre") and currently maintained in extensive grounds by the Département de la Sarthe.
* [Jardin des Plantes du Mans](/wiki/Jardin_des_Plantes_du_Mans "Jardin des Plantes du Mans")
* Musée de la reine [Bérengère](/wiki/Berengaria_of_Navarre "Berengaria of Navarre"), a museum of Le Mans history located in a gothic manor house.
* *[Musée de Tessé](/w/index.php?title=Mus%C3%A9e_de_Tess%C3%A9&action=edit&redlink=1 "Musée de Tessé (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mus%C3%A9e_de_Tess%C3%A9 "fr:Musée de Tessé")]*, the fine arts museum of the city, displaying painting (including artworks by [Philippe de Champaigne](/wiki/Philippe_de_Champaigne "Philippe de Champaigne"), [Charles Le Brun](/wiki/Charles_Le_Brun "Charles Le Brun"), [François Boucher](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Boucher "François Boucher"), [John Constable](/wiki/John_Constable "John Constable"), [Ingres](/wiki/Ingres "Ingres"), [Théodore Géricault](/wiki/Th%C3%A9odore_G%C3%A9ricault "Théodore Géricault") and [Camille Corot](/wiki/Camille_Corot "Camille Corot")) and archaeological collections as well as decorative arts.
* The [Hôtel de Ville](/wiki/H%C3%B4tel_de_Ville,_Le_Mans "Hôtel de Ville, Le Mans") (City Hall)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Climate")]

Le Mans has a temperate [oceanic climate](/wiki/Oceanic_climate "Oceanic climate") (*Cfb*) influenced by the mild Atlantic air travelling inland. Summers are warm and occasionally hot, whereas winters are mild and cloudy. Precipitation is relatively uniform and moderate year round.

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Transportation")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/Tram_Le_Mans_-_Avenue_du_G%C3%A9n%C3%A9ral_Leclerc.JPG/250px-Tram_Le_Mans_-_Avenue_du_G%C3%A9n%C3%A9ral_Leclerc.JPG)](/wiki/File:Tram_Le_Mans_-_Avenue_du_G%C3%A9n%C3%A9ral_Leclerc.JPG)

Tram in town center of Le Mans

The [Gare du Mans](/wiki/Gare_du_Mans "Gare du Mans") is the main railway station of Le Mans. It takes 1 hour to reach Paris from Le Mans by [TGV](/wiki/TGV "TGV") high speed train. There are also TGV connections to Lille, Marseille, Nantes, Rennes and Brest. Gare du Mans is also a hub for regional trains. Le Mans inaugurated a light rail system, [Le Mans tramway](/wiki/Le_Mans_tramway "Le Mans tramway") (French: *Tramway du Mans*), on 17 November 2007.[[17]](#cite_note-18)

The [Le Mans Airfield](/wiki/Le_Mans_Airfield "Le Mans Airfield") ([fr:Aéroport Le Mans-Arnage](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/A%C3%A9roport_Le_Mans-Arnage "fr:Aéroport Le Mans-Arnage") ([LME](/wiki/LME "LME") LFRM, until 1971 *Aérodrome des Raineries*) located in the [Arnage](/wiki/Arnage,_Sarthe "Arnage, Sarthe") community to the south of the City serves as regional airport.

## Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Sport")]

### Motorsport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Motorsport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Le_Mans_2007_-_Dunlop_Curve.jpg/250px-Le_Mans_2007_-_Dunlop_Curve.jpg)](/wiki/File:Le_Mans_2007_-_Dunlop_Curve.jpg)

Dunlop Curve

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Empreinte_pilotes_24h.jpg/250px-Empreinte_pilotes_24h.jpg)](/wiki/File:Empreinte_pilotes_24h.jpg)

Handprints and signatures from the winners of the 1992 edition of the [24 Hours of Le Mans](/wiki/24_Hours_of_Le_Mans "24 Hours of Le Mans"), [Mark Blundell](/wiki/Mark_Blundell "Mark Blundell"), [Derek Warwick](/wiki/Derek_Warwick "Derek Warwick"), and [Yannick Dalmas](/wiki/Yannick_Dalmas "Yannick Dalmas"), at Le Mans

The first [French Grand Prix](/wiki/French_Grand_Prix "French Grand Prix") took place on a 64-mile (103 km) *Circuit de la Sarthe* based outside of Le Mans [in 1906](/wiki/1906_French_Grand_Prix "1906 French Grand Prix").

Since the introduction of the [24 Hours of Le Mans](/wiki/24_Hours_of_Le_Mans "24 Hours of Le Mans") [sports car endurance race](/wiki/Sports_car_racing "Sports car racing") in 1923, the city has been best known for its connection with motorsports. The [Circuit des 24 Heures du Mans](/wiki/Circuit_des_24_Heures_du_Mans "Circuit des 24 Heures du Mans") originally used public roads to the South of the city leading to [Mulsanne](/wiki/Mulsanne "Mulsanne"). Some parts were replaced by permanent race track sections. The [Bugatti Circuit](/wiki/Circuit_de_la_Sarthe#Bugatti_Circuit "Circuit de la Sarthe") (named after [Ettore Bugatti](/wiki/Ettore_Bugatti "Ettore Bugatti"), founder of the [car company](/wiki/Bugatti "Bugatti") bearing his name), a relatively short permanent circuit without public roads, can be used for racing throughout the year and regularly hosts the [French motorcycle Grand Prix](/wiki/French_motorcycle_Grand_Prix "French motorcycle Grand Prix").

The "[Le Mans start](/wiki/Le_Mans_start "Le Mans start")" was used in the 24-hour race until 1969: drivers lined up across the track from their cars, ran across the track, jumped into their cars, started them, and began the race. The use of safety belts prevented this. The drivers have to be properly strapped in by mechanics.

The [1955 Le Mans disaster](/wiki/1955_Le_Mans_disaster "1955 Le Mans disaster") was a large accident during the race that killed eighty-four spectators.

### Basketball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Basketball")]

The city is home to [Le Mans Sarthe Basket](/wiki/Le_Mans_Sarthe_Basket "Le Mans Sarthe Basket"), 2006 Champion of the [LNB Pro A](/wiki/LNB_Pro_A "LNB Pro A"), France's top professional basketball division.

The team plays its home games at the [Antarès](/wiki/Antar%C3%A8s "Antarès"), which served as one of the host arenas of the FIBA [EuroBasket 1999](/wiki/EuroBasket_1999 "EuroBasket 1999").

## Gastronomy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Gastronomy")]

The culinary specialty of Le Mans is *[rillettes](/wiki/Rillettes "Rillettes")*, a shredded pork [pâté](/wiki/P%C3%A2t%C3%A9 "Pâté").

## Landmarks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Landmarks")]

Located at Mayet near Le Mans, the [Le Mans-Mayet transmitter](/wiki/Transmitter_Le_Mans-Mayet "Transmitter Le Mans-Mayet") has a height of 342 m and is one of the tallest radio masts in France.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a6/PanoramaLeMans.JPG/960px-PanoramaLeMans.JPG)](/wiki/File:PanoramaLeMans.JPG "Panorama of Le Mans, facing north-west")

Panorama of Le Mans, facing north-west

## Representation in popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Le_Mans&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Representation in popular culture")]

* Le Mans has been a setting for numerous feature films that feature its famous race.[[19]](#cite_note-20)
* Le Mans is a setting for sections of the 2020 novel, *The Invisible Life of Addie LaRue*, by V.E. Schwab.[[20]](#cite_note-21)
